name: Space Oddity Painting Dupe
version_notes: The original infinite sell was patched in 1.3, but a variation using drop/pickup still works in some versions. In 2.0+, the painting sells for less but the quest reward itself is still valuable.
description: The Space Oddity side gig rewards a painting worth ~4,000 eddies that could previously be sold and re-purchased infinitely from drop points for massive profit.
steps: Complete the Space Oddity gig (found via an NCPD scanner hustle in Rancho Coronado, Santo Domingo).
Obtain the painting 'Untitled 18 - Brancesi, 2021' from the quest reward.
Sell the painting to any drop point for ~4,000 eddies.
In older versions: re-open the drop point and buy it back for ~5 eddies, then resell. Repeat.
In current versions: the painting still sells for a good amount as a one-time reward.
notes: One of the most famous exploits in the game's history. Even post-patch, completing Space Oddity is worth it for the eddies.

name: Drop/Pickup Item Duplication
version_notes: Works inconsistently across patches. The timing window has been tightened in 2.0+ but some players report success.
description: Duplicate items by dropping them and picking them up with precise timing, exploiting an inventory sync delay.
steps: Open your inventory and select a stackable item (components, consumables, or ammo).
Drop the item on the ground.
Immediately quicksave before the item fully spawns on the ground.
Load the quicksave. In some versions, the item will be both in your inventory and on the ground.
Pick up the ground item to have double the quantity.
Repeat as needed.
notes: Results vary by platform and game version. Save before attempting so you do not lose items if it fails. Works best with crafting components.

name: NCPD Scanner Hustle Grind
version_notes: Fully intended game mechanic. Works on all versions.
description: Systematically clear all NCPD Scanner Hustles across Night City for consistent money, XP, and Street Cred. Not an exploit but an efficient farming strategy.
steps: Open the map and filter for NCPD Scanner Hustles (blue skull icons).
Start in Watson — it has the densest cluster of low-level encounters.
Clear each encounter, loot all enemies and containers.
Sell all looted weapons and clothing at the nearest drop point or vendor.
Move to the next district: Westbrook, Heywood, Santo Domingo, Pacifica, Badlands.
Skip time 24 hours if vendors run out of money, then continue selling.
notes: This is the most reliable and risk-free money method. Combining it with access point breaching doubles your income per area.

name: Gig Reset Farming
version_notes: Most gig reset methods were patched in 2.0. Some repeatable NCPD encounters still reset after extended time skipping.
description: Force gigs and open-world encounters to respawn by manipulating save/load or time skipping, allowing repeated farming of XP and rewards.
steps: Complete a gig or NCPD encounter normally.
Travel far from the encounter location (at least 500m away).
Skip time by 48-72 hours using the wait function.
Return to the encounter location to check if it has respawned.
If not, save, quit to main menu, reload, and check again.
notes: NCPD encounters are more likely to reset than fixer gigs. Gigs themselves do not respawn — this primarily works for minor encounters.

name: Access Point Breach Farming
version_notes: Fully working mechanic in all versions. Perk investments increase rewards.
description: Systematically breach every access point in Night City for direct eddie extraction. With Intelligence perks, each access point yields massive returns.
steps: Invest in Intelligence attribute (at least 10 points recommended).
Unlock the Advanced Datamine perk for increased breach rewards.
Scan for access points in every location (wall jacks, antennas, server racks).
Complete the Breach Protocol minigame — aim for all three daemon sequences.
Completing all three sequences in one breach maximizes your eddie payout.
Combine with NCPD hustle farming for maximum efficiency.
notes: Each access point can yield 800-3,000+ eddies with the right perks. There are hundreds across Night City. This is a legitimate game mechanic, not an exploit.

name: Vendor Cash Refresh via Skip Time
version_notes: Intended game behavior. Works across all versions and platforms.
description: Reset vendor cash reserves by skipping time 24 hours, allowing you to sell more loot continuously without switching vendors.
steps: Sell items to a vendor until their eddies are depleted.
Exit the vendor menu.
Open the time skip menu and skip 24 hours.
Re-open the vendor — their cash has been refreshed to the default amount.
Continue selling your remaining loot.
Repeat as needed.
notes: Vendor cash caps at roughly 20,000 eddies. This pairs perfectly with NCPD grinding and crafting loops. Some vendors have higher cash reserves than others.

name: Free Rayfield Caliburn
version_notes: Works in all game versions. The car permanently adds to your garage.
description: Obtain the Rayfield Caliburn — one of the fastest and most expensive cars in the game — completely free from a hidden tunnel in the Badlands.
steps: Complete the Ghost Town main quest with Panam (Act 2).
Wait at least three in-game days after completing the quest.
Travel to the tunnel where you rescued Hellman during the quest (south of the solar farm in the Badlands).
Drive or walk into the tunnel and look for a parked black Rayfield Caliburn near shipping containers.
Approach the car and press the interaction prompt to claim it.
The car is now permanently added to your vehicle collection.
notes: The Caliburn is worth over 150,000 eddies if purchased from a fixer. This is the same model driven by Dexter DeShaw. It is the second-fastest car in the game.

name: Boundary Skip Exploration
version_notes: Many boundary skips were patched in 2.0, but some still work using double jump and specific geometry.
description: Use double jump or charge jump cyberware combined with specific terrain geometry to access areas outside the intended play boundaries, including unfinished areas and hidden locations.
steps: Equip Reinforced Tendons (double jump) or Fortified Ankles (charge jump).
Look for low walls, sloped surfaces, or geometry near the edges of accessible areas.
Double jump while running at angled geometry to gain extra height or clip past barriers.
Some known spots: the dam wall east of the Badlands, rooftops in Corpo Plaza, and certain coastline areas.
Explore out-of-bounds areas — some contain unused assets, terrain, and interesting geometry.
notes: Out-of-bounds areas may lack collision, causing you to fall through the world. Quicksave frequently. Some boundary areas contain developer test objects.

name: Iconic Weapon Upgrade Cost Exploit
version_notes: The specific exploit varies by patch. In 2.0+, iconic upgrading is streamlined but some cost-reduction tricks remain.
description: Reduce the component cost of upgrading iconic weapons by manipulating your crafting level or using specific perk combinations to minimize material requirements.
steps: Invest in Technical Ability and crafting-related perks.
Unlock perks that reduce crafting component costs.
Save before upgrading an iconic weapon.
Upgrade the weapon to the next tier and check the component cost.
If the cost seems too high, reload and try leveling your crafting skill slightly higher to unlock better efficiency perks.
Some perks provide percentage-based component reduction — stack these before major upgrades.
notes: Always save before upgrading iconics. You cannot reverse an upgrade, and component costs at higher tiers are steep without optimization.

name: Crafting Profit Loop
version_notes: Profit margins were reduced in 2.0 but the loop still works. Pre-2.0 allowed nearly infinite profit via green item spam.
description: Buy cheap crafting components from vendors, craft higher-tier weapons or items, and sell the finished products for more than the component cost, creating a profit loop.
steps: Purchase common and uncommon crafting components from multiple vendors.
Open the crafting menu and craft the highest-tier weapon you can (Nekomata, Copperhead, or similar).
Sell the crafted weapons to a vendor or drop point.
Compare the sale price to your component cost — you should profit 50-200% per item depending on perks.
Use the vendor skip time trick to refresh vendor cash and component stock.
Repeat the buy-craft-sell cycle.
notes: This also levels your Crafting skill simultaneously. Higher crafting skill unlocks better items with larger profit margins. Best combined with selling perks.

name: Vending Machine Exploit
version_notes: The original $10 buy / $750 sell was patched in 1.2. Minor vending machine profit tricks remain in some versions.
description: Purchase cheap items from vending machines and sell or disassemble them for profit due to pricing discrepancies between buy and sell values.
steps: Find a vending machine selling consumables for 10 eddies each.
Buy the maximum amount of a single item.
In older versions: sell these items to a drop point for significantly more than purchase price.
In current versions: disassemble the items for crafting components, then craft higher-value items to sell.
The component value from mass-disassembly can exceed the purchase cost with the right perks.
notes: Much less effective post-patch. The disassembly route is more reliable than direct reselling in current versions.

name: Double Reward Quicksave Trick
version_notes: Timing-dependent. Works inconsistently after 2.0 but some players report success on specific quest reward screens.
description: Quicksave at the exact moment a quest reward is granted to receive the reward twice — once from the trigger and once from the save reload.
steps: Identify a quest or gig that is about to give a reward (eddies, item, or XP).
As the reward notification appears on screen, immediately quicksave (F5 on PC).
Load the quicksave.
In some cases, the reward is applied on save and then re-applied on the quest completion trigger upon load.
Check your inventory or eddie balance to confirm duplication.
This works best on gig completion rewards from fixers.
notes: Very inconsistent. The timing window is extremely tight. Does not work on all quest types. Save scumming may be needed.

name: Cyber Engine Tweaks (CET) Console
version_notes: CET is updated for each game version by the modding community. Requires local file access.
description: Cyber Engine Tweaks is a modding framework that adds a developer console to the game, allowing you to execute Lua commands to add items, eddies, teleport, change stats, and more.
steps: Download CET from the Nexus Mods website.
Extract the mod files into your Cyberpunk 2077 installation directory (bin/x64/plugins/).
Launch the game and press the tilde key (~) to open the CET console.
Use commands like: Game.AddToInventory('Items.money', 100000) to add eddies.
Use Game.GetPlayer():SetLevel(60) to set your level.
Browse the CET wiki for the full command list.
notes: Requires local PC installation — cannot be used on GeForce Now, Stadia, or consoles. CET is the foundation for most Cyberpunk mods.

name: WeMod Trainer
version_notes: WeMod updates trainers for each game patch. Requires local installation and WeMod app.
description: WeMod is a third-party trainer application that provides toggleable cheats like infinite health, unlimited ammo, super speed, one-hit kills, and free crafting.
steps: Download and install WeMod from wemod.com.
Search for Cyberpunk 2077 in the WeMod library.
Launch the game through WeMod or attach WeMod to the running process.
Toggle cheats on/off using the WeMod overlay or assigned hotkeys.
Available cheats include: Infinite Health, Infinite Stamina, No Reload, Unlimited Eddies, Max Street Cred, and more.
notes: WeMod requires a local game installation — it cannot attach to cloud-streamed games. Some antivirus software may flag WeMod. The free version has limited features.

name: Cheat Engine Memory Editing
version_notes: Cheat Engine tables are community-maintained. Requires local installation and advanced knowledge.
description: Cheat Engine is a memory scanner/editor that can modify in-game values like health, eddies, attribute points, and perk points by directly editing memory addresses.
steps: Download Cheat Engine from cheatengine.org.
Download a Cyberpunk 2077 cheat table (.CT file) from the Cheat Engine forums or Fearless Revolution.
Launch Cyberpunk 2077 and attach Cheat Engine to the process.
Load the cheat table and enable desired scripts (infinite health, money editor, attribute editor, etc.).
Modify values as needed through the Cheat Engine interface.
Be careful with pointer-based values — changing wrong addresses can crash the game.
notes: Requires local game installation. Cannot be used on GFN. Risk of game crashes if incorrect memory addresses are modified. Always save before using.

name: FLiNG Trainer
version_notes: FLiNG updates trainers within days of game patches. Requires local installation.
description: FLiNG provides standalone trainer executables for Cyberpunk 2077 with toggleable cheats including god mode, infinite resources, super damage, and stat editing.
steps: Download the Cyberpunk 2077 FLiNG trainer from flingtrainer.com.
Run the trainer executable as administrator.
Launch Cyberpunk 2077.
Use the assigned hotkeys to toggle cheats (displayed in the trainer window).
Common options: Numpad 1 for Infinite Health, Numpad 2 for Infinite Stamina, Numpad 5 for Add Eddies.
notes: FLiNG trainers are version-specific — download the trainer matching your game version. Cannot be used on cloud gaming platforms. Some antivirus may flag the executable.

name: Save File Hex Editing
version_notes: Save structure changes between major patches. Community tools like CP2077 Save Editor are more user-friendly.
description: Directly edit Cyberpunk 2077 save files using a hex editor or dedicated save editor tool to modify attributes, inventory, quest states, and more.
steps: Locate your save files: %USERPROFILE%\Saved Games\CD Projekt Red\Cyberpunk 2077.
Make a backup of the save folder before editing.
Download a save editor tool (like CP2077 Save Editor from GitHub) for a user-friendly interface.
Open the save file and navigate to the section you want to modify (inventory, stats, etc.).
Make changes and save the file.
Load the modified save in-game to verify changes.
notes: Requires access to local save files — not possible on GFN without downloading cloud saves. Incorrect edits can corrupt saves. Always keep backups.

name: DLL Injection Modding
version_notes: DLL-based mods require specific game versions. Often break with updates.
description: Inject custom DLL files into the game process to modify behavior, bypass restrictions, or add features not available through normal modding frameworks.
steps: Download the desired DLL mod (e.g., custom script loaders, performance patches).
Place the DLL file in the appropriate game directory (usually bin/x64/ or bin/x64/plugins/).
Some DLLs require a loader like Ultimate ASI Loader or CET to inject properly.
Launch the game — the DLL will be loaded automatically if placed correctly.
Check the mod's documentation for configuration options (usually in an INI file).
notes: DLL injection is blocked on cloud platforms and consoles. Incompatible DLLs can cause crashes or anti-cheat flags (if applicable). Only use trusted sources.

name: Nexus Mods (Full Modding)
version_notes: Nexus Mods has the largest Cyberpunk 2077 mod library. New mods are added daily. Requires local installation.
description: Install gameplay-altering mods from Nexus Mods including new weapons, vehicles, appearance changes, gameplay overhauls, UI improvements, and cheat mods.
steps: Create a free account on nexusmods.com.
Install Vortex Mod Manager (Nexus's official tool) or use manual installation.
Search for Cyberpunk 2077 mods — popular categories: Gameplay, Cheats, Visuals, Weapons.
Download mods and install via Vortex (one-click install) or extract manually to the game directory.
Most mods require CET or REDmod framework — install these first.
Launch the game and verify mods are working. Check for conflicts if issues arise.
notes: Full modding requires local PC installation. GFN does not support custom mods. Console players have no mod support. Always read mod descriptions for compatibility notes.

name: Debug Mode Activation
version_notes: Debug mode access was removed in later patches. CET console replaces most debug functionality.
description: Activate the game's built-in debug/developer mode to access hidden menus, teleportation, spawning, and diagnostic tools originally used by CD Projekt Red during development.
steps: In early game versions (pre-1.2), debug mode could be activated via specific launch parameters or config edits.
In current versions, install Cyber Engine Tweaks which provides equivalent functionality.
Use CET console commands to access debug-style features: teleportation, NPC spawning, time control.
Some debug features are accessible through modded menu mods available on Nexus Mods.
The full debug menu is no longer accessible without significant game file modification.
notes: Direct debug mode access was removed by CDPR. CET provides nearly identical functionality for PC players. Not available on consoles or cloud platforms.

name: Memory Editors & Scanners
version_notes: Generic memory editors work on any game version. Addresses change between patches.
description: Use third-party memory editing tools (beyond Cheat Engine) such as ArtMoney, GameGuardian, or Process Hacker to scan and modify game memory values in real time.
steps: Install a memory editor of your choice on your local PC.
Launch Cyberpunk 2077 and note a specific value you want to change (e.g., current eddies: 15,000).
Attach the memory editor to the Cyberpunk2077.exe process.
Search for the known value (15000). Multiple results will appear.
Go back to the game and change the value slightly (spend 100 eddies, now 14,900).
Search again for the new value (14900) to narrow down the exact memory address.
Modify the found address to your desired value.
notes: Requires local installation. Time-consuming compared to trainers or CET. Risk of modifying wrong addresses causing crashes. Good for learning how games store data.

name: Game File Modifications
version_notes: File structure changes with major patches. Community modding tools keep up with changes.
description: Directly modify game configuration files, archives, and scripts to change gameplay values, unlock hidden features, or customize the experience beyond what mods offer.
steps: Navigate to the Cyberpunk 2077 installation directory.
Back up any files before modifying them.
For simple tweaks: edit files in the r6/config/ and engine/config/ directories (INI-style settings).
For deeper changes: use WolvenKit (the community modding toolkit) to extract, modify, and repack game archives.
Modify weapon stats, NPC behavior, economy values, or visual settings at the source level.
Repack modified files and place them in the mod directory.
notes: Requires local installation and technical knowledge. WolvenKit is the standard tool for archive modding. Game updates may overwrite or break modifications.

name: Photo Mode Boundary Exploits
version_notes: Some photo mode camera boundary tricks still work in 2.0+. The free camera range was limited in patches but not fully restricted.
description: Use photo mode's free camera to scout areas, see through walls, and explore restricted zones without moving V's actual position.
steps: Enter photo mode from the pause menu during gameplay.
Detach the camera from V and move it freely through the environment.
The camera can pass through some walls and barriers that block normal movement.
Use this to scout enemy positions in missions, find hidden items, or explore inaccessible areas.
Note that V does not actually move — this is observation only.
The camera has a limited range from V's position, but it is generous enough to scout most nearby areas.
notes: Works on all platforms including GFN. The camera cannot interact with anything — strictly visual scouting. Useful for planning stealth approaches.

name: Quicksave Manipulation
version_notes: Basic save scumming always works. Some specific exploits involving save/load during combat or dialogue were tightened in 2.0.
description: Use quicksave and quickload to manipulate RNG outcomes, retry skill checks, get better loot drops, and recover from failed stealth or combat encounters.
steps: Quicksave (F5) before any risky encounter, dialogue choice, or loot container.
If the outcome is unfavorable, quickload (F9) to retry.
For loot: save before opening a container, load if the item is not what you want. Some containers have randomized loot.
For combat: save before engaging to test different approaches.
For dialogue: save before critical story choices to see different outcomes.
Note that some quickhack and combat RNG is seeded — you may need to change your approach slightly to get different results on reload.
notes: Basic save scumming works everywhere including GFN. Some advanced manipulation (like forcing specific loot rolls) was patched to use seeded RNG.

name: AI Pathing Exploits
version_notes: AI was significantly improved in the 2.0 update. Many pre-2.0 pathing exploits no longer work. Some geometry-based exploits remain.
description: Exploit enemy AI pathfinding limitations to fight enemies from positions they cannot reach or attack from, making difficult encounters trivial.
steps: Find elevated positions that enemies cannot path to (rooftops, scaffolding, certain ledges).
Use double jump to reach these positions during or before combat.
Enemies will attempt to path to you but may get stuck or loop between waypoints.
Attack from safety using ranged weapons or quickhacks.
Some doorways and narrow passages cause AI to bottleneck — use grenades and area attacks.
Post-2.0, enemies are smarter but still struggle with extreme verticality and certain geometry.
notes: AI improvements in 2.0 made this less reliable. Enemies now use grenades to flush you out and netrunners can hack you from distance. Still useful for specific encounters.
